toolchain.firtool: CIRCT firtool-1.135.0 (auto-resolved by firtool-resolver from Chisel 7.3.0)

contents.flat/DefaultConfig.flat.sv.gz: single-file XSTop, gzipped (2009 modules); see note on size below
contents.filelist.f: the generator's own filelist -- INCOMPLETE, see notes
contents.meta/git-provenance.txt: git log/diff header emitted by the XiangShan Makefile

notes: Built via 'make verilog' (FPGATOP=top.TopMain) -> XSTop, the SoC top. This is NOT SimTop: no difftest comparison harness.

filelist.f is INCOMPLETE: it omits ClockGate.sv and all DiffExt*.v. The DiffExt*.v modules ARE instantiated (by DummyDPICWrapper_* modules that filelist.f does list), so compiling filelist.f alone leaves undefined modules. Compile all of rtl/ instead, or use flat/.
SRAMs are behavioral models (ram_*.sv). Fine for simulation/formal; not synthesizable to a PDK without SRAM replacement.
flat/ is gzipped because the raw file is 101-155 MB, over GitHub's 100 MB per-file limit. gunzip -k flat/DefaultConfig.flat.sv.gz to use it.
XSTop.fir (FIRRTL, 1.0-1.5 GB raw) is intentionally not frozen. Regenerate with the build_command above if needed.
Verified: verilator --lint-only --top-module XSTop on flat/ reports 0 errors.

simulation.tool: verilator 5.022, standalone testbench (sim/tb_xstop.sv) driving XSTop with an AXI4 DRAM model
simulation.result: PARTIAL — XSTop builds into a cycle-accurate simulator, comes out of reset, and fetches the reset-vector cachelines (0x80000000/40/80/c0) correctly through its L1I/L2/AXI hierarchy, but does not retire to WFI standalone.
simulation.evidence: sim/results/xiangshan_MinimalConfig_XSTop.log: 4 memory read bursts at the reset vector, then no commit/store/further-fetch. Reset duration (200 vs 5000 cycles) does not change it; no peripheral polling occurs.
simulation.why: XSTop is XiangShan's FPGA-platform SoC top and expects XiangShan's own boot environment (SimTop/difftest or the FPGA harness) to complete bring-up; a bare AXI memory is not sufficient to drive it to instruction retirement.
simulation.note: This is a limitation of standalone bring-up, not of the frozen RTL: the fetch path is proven to elaborate and function. Rocket/BOOM use chipyard's TestHarness+fesvr, which handles bring-up, hence full ISA-test execution there.
